'''Llokay''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], and later a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] in the years leading up to the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]. Llokay trained in the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and both he and his sister [[Yevra]] were fiercely loyal to the strongest of the students, a fellow Zabrak named [[Sirak]]. In the year [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], Llokay and all the other Sith apprentices in the academy were promoted due to the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]' need for Sith Lords on the front lines of the [[New Sith Wars|war]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. A short time after Llokay's elevation, he, Yevra, and Sirak set a [[Duel in the Korriban Academy|trap to eliminate]] a rival Sith, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Bane]], who had defeated, humiliated, and injured Sirak a short time before. However, the three were betrayed by a co-conspirator, [[Githany]], who armed and fought alongside Bane in the confrontation. Llokay and Yevra were killed by Githany's [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]], and Sirak was killed shortly thereafter by Bane.

==Biography==
At some point, Llokay, a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]], and his sister [[Yevra]] were found and recognized as [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] by the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. They were considered strong enough to train at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where only those with the potential to become [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Masters]] were enrolled. There, they met the strongest of the apprentices, a fellow Zabrak known as [[Sirak]]. The siblings became completely loyal to Sirak and were still in training there by the year [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. In that year, a fellow apprentice named [[Darth Bane/Legends|Bane]] challenged Sirak to a battle during their combat training lesson presided over by [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Kas'im]], a Sith Master. Upon the issuance of Bane's challenge, Llokay and Yevra pushed their way to the front of the crowd of students that encircled the dueling ring, both of them showing conspicuous eagerness at the fight to come. Sirak, wielding a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed]] [[Sith training saber]], quickly and easily defeated and injured Bane.<ref name="pod" />

Some time later, after training in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] with fellow Sith [[Githany]], combat training with Lord Kas'im, and many hours reading the ancient tomes in the Sith academy's library, Bane challenged Sirak once again. Before the duel commenced, Sirak handed Llokay his cloak, which he held for him during the duration of the battle. This time Bane defeated and disfigured Sirak, yet hesitated when he had the opportunity to land a killing blow. Sirak spent time in a [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] tank, and was approached by Githany, who plotted with him to kill Bane. At the time Bane was on an excursion to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] to investigate the ancient Sith tombs there, and the two agreed to attack Bane once he returned. During the period in between, one of the Sith Masters in charge of the [[New Sith Wars|war effort]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Lord [[Skere Kaan|Kaan]], ordered all the students on Korriban to report to the front lines on the planet of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] and promoted them all to the title of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Llokay, suddenly a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], was given a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="pod" />

Bane finally returned from his journey to the Valley of the Dark Lords before the Sith students were to leave for Ruusan, and the trap was set. Llokay, along with Sirak and Yevra waited in the library upon hearing of his return. Githany lured Bane, who had left his lightsaber in his personal chamber, into the archives where the Zabraks waited. Llokay, Yevra, and Sirak moved to [[Duel in the Korriban Academy|attack]] Bane, but found themselves betrayed by Githany, who supplied Bane with her lightsaber. Githany herself was armed with a [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]], which she quickly used to kill Yevra. Llokay, overwhelmed by the grief and rage caused by his sister's death, ferociously attacked Bane, driving him back several steps while Githany cracked her lightwhip at Llokay's eyes and face. For a time, Llokay was able to continue the attack on Bane while warding off Githany's lightwhip strikes, but lost an eye after misjudging the erratic path of Githany's whip. Screaming in pain, Llokay was struck again in the throat, searing his vocal cords, and causing him to die in agonized silence. Sirak was killed shortly after, decapitated by Bane's blade after begging for mercy.<ref name="pod" />

==Personality and traits==
Both Llokay and his sister Yevra were utterly and completely loyal to Sirak, who was generally regarded as the strongest of the Korriban apprentices, and carried his personal items while he was fighting. Upon watching his sister die by the hand of Githany, Llokay was overwhelmed by grief and rage, which he channeled into a ferocious assault on Bane before dying from a strike by Githany's lightwhip. Both Llokay and his sister had red skin.<ref name="pod" />

==Behind the scenes==
Llokay and his sister Yevra made their first appearance in [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s [[2006]] novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''. So far, it is his only appearance in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]], although in [[2008]] Llokay was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
